full_text:
School Timings Changed In Jharkhand Amid Heat Wave Alert
Jharkhand revises school timings from April 21 amid heat wave alert. Check new schedule, affected districts, and IMD advisory for students.
The Jharkhand government has revised school timings across the state due to rising temperatures and heat wave-like conditions. The decision, effective from April 21, aims to safeguard students from extreme weather, particularly during peak daytime heat.
According to officials, the updated schedule will apply to all government, aided, unaided, and private schools until further notice. This move comes after temperatures surged across several districts, prompting immediate action.
New School Timings in Jharkhand: Classes to End Earlier
As per the education department’s directive, younger students from kindergarten to Class 8 will attend school from 7:00 AM to 11:30 AM. Meanwhile, students from Classes 9 to 12 will continue their classes until 12:00 noon.
Earlier, many schools operated beyond noon, exposing students to intense heat. The revised schedule is intended to minimise health risks and ensure safer learning conditions during the ongoing heat spell.
Heat Wave Alert Issued by IMD Across Districts
The India Meteorological Department has issued a ‘yellow’ alert for heat wave conditions from April 20 to April 22 in several parts of the state.
Districts currently experiencing heat wave-like conditions include Garhwa, Palamu, Chatra, Latehar, Bokaro, and Dhanbad. The alert is expected to extend to additional districts such as Simdega, Saraikela-Kharswan, and East and West Singhbhum.
The Ranchi Meteorological Centre has warned of continued high temperatures, urging precautionary measures.
Temperature Surge Across Jharkhand: Key Readings
Several regions have recorded extreme temperatures in recent days. Daltonganj reported the highest temperature at 43.9°C, followed by Bokaro at 43.1°C. Ranchi recorded a comparatively lower but still high temperature of 39.3°C.
These rising temperatures have raised concerns about heat-related illnesses, especially among school children.
Government Steps & Advisory for Public Safety
State Health and Disaster Management Minister Irfan Ansari has called for the change in school timings considering the ongoing weather conditions.
He has instructed officials to remain vigilant and ensure that citizens, particularly children, are protected from the heat wave.
The weather department has advised residents to stay hydrated, wear light-coloured cotton clothes, and cover their heads with a hat, cloth, or umbrella when stepping outdoors.

full_text:
Clear, dry weather to persist across Jharkhand:Heatwave warning issued for several regions; no rainfall expected
- Copy link
The weather across Jharkhand is expected to remain clear and dry today, with no possibility of rainfall, according to the India Meteorological Department. The sky will remain mostly clear in most parts of the state, leading to strong sunlight and a sharper heat effect during the day.
While some regions experienced light cloud cover and brief showers on the previous day, conditions have now stabilised. No significant change in temperature is expected over the next 24 hours, and dry weather is likely to persist.
Heatwave likely in north-western, southern and central districts
The weather department has issued a heatwave warning for parts of north-western, southern and central Jharkhand. Districts such as Bokaro and Dhanbad are expected to experience hot winds and rising temperatures.
Residents have been advised to avoid stepping out during peak afternoon hours and to take precautions against heat exposure. No alerts for thunderstorms or strong winds have been issued, but continued high temperatures mean little relief from the heat.
Ranchi to record around 39°C amid clear skies
In the state capital Ranchi, the weather will remain dry with clear skies. The maximum temperature is expected to hover around 39°C, while the minimum may settle near 23°C. Prolonged sunshine is likely to increase discomfort due to heat and humidity.
Similar weather conditions are expected to continue for the next two to three days, with no immediate relief in sight. Experts have advised people to stay hydrated and limit exposure to direct sunlight.
Palamu hottest at 43.9°C; several districts above normal
Palamu recorded the highest temperature in the state over the past 24 hours at 43.9°C, around 4 degrees above normal. Other high readings include:
Ranchi recorded 39.3°C, which is 3.4 degrees above normal, indicating a widespread rise in temperatures across the state.
Temperatures may touch 44°C in several districts
The forecast indicates that maximum temperatures could reach 44°C in districts such as Chatra, Garhwa and Palamu. Koderma may see temperatures up to 43°C.
In East and West Singhbhum and Saraikela-Kharsawan, temperatures are expected to range between 42°C and 44°C, accompanied by hot winds and heatwave-like conditions.
Other regions, including Ranchi, Bokaro, Ramgarh and Hazaribag, are likely to record maximum temperatures between 39°C and 43°C. In north-eastern districts like Deoghar, Dumka, Giridih and Jamtara, temperatures may rise up to 40–42°C.
Minimum temperatures across the state are expected to remain between 22°C and 27°C, contributing to persistent heat even at night. The Meteorological Department has indicated no significant drop in temperatures over the next five days.

full_text:
धनबाद में गर्मी चरम पर, येलो अलर्ट के बीच वेस्टर्न डिस्टर्बेंस से बदलेगा मौसम
Dhanbad Weather Update: धनबाद में गर्मी का कहर जारी है, पारा 41 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक पहुंच गया है। मौसम विभाग ने हीट वेव का येलो अलर्ट जारी किया है, जिस ...और पढ़ें
जागरण संवाददाता, धनबाद। Dhanbad Weather News अप्रैल का दूसरा पखवाड़ा तपता ही जा रहा है। सुबह सूर्याेदय के बाद से ही धूप की तपिश बढ़ रही है। दोपहर में लू जैसे हालात बन रहे हैं तो रात में गर्म हवा के थपेड़े झकझोर रहे हैं। धनबाद का पारा 41 डिग्री पार कर जा रहा है।
सोमवार को धनबाद शहर का का पारा 41 डिग्री पर रहा। जिससे जन जीवन प्रभावित रहा। आज मंगवार को पारा एक डिग्री और ऊपर चढ़ सकता है। यानी पारा 42 डिग्री रह सकता है जिससे प्रचंड गर्मी झेलनी होगी। मौसम विभाग ने धनबाद के साथ ही बोकारो (Bokaro Weather) और गिरिडीह (Giridih Weather) में 22 अप्रैल तक उष्ण लहर यानी हीट वेव चलने का येलो अलर्ट जारी किया है।
23 से फिर सक्रिय होगा नया पश्चिमी विक्षोभ
मौसम विभाग के अनुसार, 23 अप्रैल से फिर एक नया पश्चिमी विक्षोभ सक्रिय हो सकता है। इससे मौसम के करवट लेने की उम्मीद है। फरवरी-मार्च और अप्रैल के पहले पखवाड़े तक पश्चिमी विक्षोभ के कारण ही आंधी-बारिश का दौर जारी था, जिससे गर्मी बेअसर थी।
पश्चिमी विक्षोभ थमते ही भीषण गर्मी ने असर दिखाना शुरू किया। अब एक बार फिर राहत मिलने की उम्मीद है। इसके प्रभाव से 24 से 26 अप्रैल के बीच बादल छाने तथा गर्जन के साथ आंधी-बारिश की संभावना है।
